channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Northstar commuter trains from Minneapolis to Big Lake could be replaced with bus service
item.description: After more than 15 years, the days of Northstar commuter rail service between Minneapolis and Big Lake may be numbered. MnDOT and the Met Council are considering replacing Northstar commuter trains through the northwest Twin Cities metro area with buses.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Irish leaders boycott Washington St Patrick's Day events over Trump's Gaza comments
item.description: Sinn Féin leader Mary Lou McDonald and Northern Ireland First Minister Michelle O’Neill will skip St. Patrick’s Day in the U.S. this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tax cut plan hits turbulence ahead of House vote as Republicans split
item.description: House and Senate Republicans are working to advance dueling versions of their budget reconciliation bills.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge blocks DOGE from accessing Education Department records
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland issued a temporary restraining order against the Department of Government Efficiency's access to personal information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump deploys SpongeBob meme to troll DOGE critics
item.description: Elon Musk and President Donald Trump shared a SpongeBob SquarePants-themed meme after Musk announced government workers would receive an email seeking information about what they accomplished last week
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Roosevelt Hotel to shut down as migrant arrival center: NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – The migrant arrival center at the Roosevelt Hotel in Manhattan is set to shut down, New York City Mayor Eric Adams will announce on Monday. More than 173,000 migrants have gone through the site since it opened in May 2023, according to city data.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Millionaire Vivek Ramaswamy set to join the Ohio governor's race
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Vivek Ramaswamy, the Cincinnati-born biotech entrepreneur who departed the Department of Government Efficiency initiative on President Donald Trump's first day, was expected to launch his bid for Ohio governor Monday.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed bill may criminalize landlords, property management for unpaid utilities
item.description: Jackson says he was inspired to write the bill after dilapidated apartments, like the Jolie and Woodlawn Terrace Apartments, forced law-abiding tenants out of their homes.
item.dc:creator: Myriam Samake, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump directs ICE agents to find, deport unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: More than 600,000 immigrant children have crossed the U.S.-Mexico border without a parent or legal guardian since 2019.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: States threaten fines and jail time for local officials who resist Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Republican state lawmakers seeking to aid President Donald Trump’s crackdown on illegal immigration are threatening local officials who resist with lawsuits, fines and even potential jail time.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illinois bill would create state's own DOGE to eliminate wasteful spending
item.description: Illinois is one of more than a dozen states suing the Trump administration over the federal DOGE agency.
item.dc:creator: John Clark, NewsNation

item.title: Many Americans don't trust the media to cover Trump: Survey
item.description: Most Americans in a new survey said they have little confidence that the news media can cover President Trump with fairness and accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's what to know about Germany's election this Sunday
item.description: An economic slump, an immigration crisis and the lifting of a security blanket provided for decades by the U.S. are issues on the minds of German voters. The far-right AfD is polling in second place.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz, MPR News

item.title: Ex-Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio arrested near Capitol
item.description: Enrique Tarrio, the former national chairman of the Proud Boys, was arrested by U.S. Capitol Police on Friday following an altercation with a counter protester.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: PIX Panel discusses Hochul’s safeguard plan for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Thursday announced proposed safeguards to ensure that New York City is operating in the best interest of its residents.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Union workers written up, fired for using sick days; Nevada lawmaker moves to close loophole
item.description: Some union workers are afraid to use sick days because they might be written up or fired for being "undependable," and now a Nevada lawmaker is coming to their aid.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: ‘In Trump We Trust’ Is Exploding
item.description: President Trump forced through his Cabinet – we told you that would only increase his control over the Republican Party, and it did. To that point, Matt Lewis headlines in The Hill, “Conservatism used to mean something. Now it’s just whatever Trump says.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: After mass layoffs, some federal agencies are trying to bring employees back
item.description: A number of federal agencies, from HHS to the USDA, have laid off employees only to rescind those terminations days later. An expert says it "suggests something about mismanagement of government.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Judge cancels trial for NYC Mayor Eric Adams but leaves corruption charges intact for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cancelled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise him on how to handle the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways from Elon Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Republicans approve budget framework, pushing past Democratic objections after all-night vote
item.description: The early Friday vote was a key step toward providing money the Trump administration says it needs funds for mass deportations and security measures that top the Republican agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers to Trump: Reverse cuts to 9/11 health care
item.description: New York's senate and congressional delegations agree that federal cuts to the World Trade Center Health Program will hurt survivors of and first responders to the Sept. 11 terrorist attacks.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, PIX 11

item.title: Bill that would make more attorney general data public fails in Minnesota House vote
item.description: House Republicans pushed for a vote on a bill to require the attorney general’s office, occupied now by DFLer Keith Ellison, to be more forthcoming with records. Democrats framed it as a partisan measure.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News
